name: Bohmian mechanics (pilot-wave theory)
summary: Augments the wave function psi with definite particle positions Q evolving via a guidance equation dQ/dt = (hbar/m) Im(grad psi / psi). The wave function evolves unitarily by Schrodinger; particles always have definite positions. Reproduces standard quantum predictions (including Bell-inequality violations, by being explicitly non-local). Avoids the measurement problem because positions are always definite.

addresses_puzzles.note: Particle positions are always definite, so 'measurement' just reveals pre-existing values. Non-locality is explicit (guidance depends on positions of all particles instantaneously) but reproduces all standard QM predictions. Faces difficulties extending to relativistic QFT and to spin/identical-particle systems.

new_fields: definite particle positions Q (in addition to wave function)
introduces.note: Adds the position trajectories as ontology beyond standard QM but requires no new free parameters.

history: Bohm (1952), 'A suggested interpretation of the quantum theory in terms of "hidden" variables', Phys. Rev. 85, 166 and 180. Predates Bell's theorem (1964); Bell explicitly cites Bohm as motivation. Continues active development in foundations community; relativistic generalisations (Durr-Goldstein-Tumulka-Zanghi 2014) ongoing.

references.citation: Bohm, 'A suggested interpretation of the quantum theory in terms of "hidden" variables. I', Phys. Rev. 85, 166 (1952)
Bohm, 'A suggested interpretation of the quantum theory in terms of "hidden" variables. II', Phys. Rev. 85, 180 (1952)
